page: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is equipped with 3780 GB of local SSD storage. The processor features a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support, though it lacks AVX, AMX, and hyperthreading capabilities.

Benchmark data indicates that the D64pds_v6 excels in multi-threaded and memory-intensive workloads. It achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core metrics, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, ranking in the top tier for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read and write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang also reach top-tier levels. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier across stress-ng, Geekbench, and bzip3 decompression tests, highlighting a tradeoff where parallelized execution is significantly more performant than single-threaded tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64pds_v6 offers high resource density by combining 64 dedicated ARM cores with a substantial 3780 GB local storage allocation, making it a viable option for scale-out workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM inference tasks, demonstrating strong performance in prompt processing for small models and text generation for medium models.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, ranking in the top 10% for multi-threaded benchmarks, database operations, and software compilation. However, single-core performance is average, representing a tradeoff for non-parallelized tasks. Lacking hyperthreading and AVX extensions, it relies on physical core density and hardware AES cryptography. The platform provides high resource density for scale-out environments, making this configuration highly suited for database management, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and software development pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local SSD storage for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz
256 GB of system memory paired with 3780 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Average single-core performance, favoring highly parallelized workloads
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
